assertion_results.text: Epics are generated between Feature and Tasks — at least 2 Epics are created (one per repository) when by-repository grouping is used
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lines 14-24 define two Epics: 'Epic 1: TC-9006: trustify-backend' with Tasks 1-4 and 'Epic 2: TC-9006: trustify-ui' with Tasks 5-10. summary-comment.md line 29 confirms 'Grouping strategy: by-repository (one Epic per repository)'.

assertion_results.text: Each Epic summary follows the format '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' (e.g., 'TC-9006: trustify-backend', 'TC-9006: trustify-ui')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 14: 'Epic 1: TC-9006: trustify-backend' and line 20: 'Epic 2: TC-9006: trustify-ui'. Both follow the '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' format exactly.

assertion_results.text: Each task specifies its parent Epic key in the output — tasks targeting trustify-backend are assigned to the backend Epic, tasks targeting trustify-ui are assigned to the frontend Epic
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lines 36-43 show Tasks 1-4 under 'Epic 1: TC-9006: trustify-backend' with Repository 'trustify-backend'. Lines 47-54 show Tasks 5-10 under 'Epic 2: TC-9006: trustify-ui' with Repository 'trustify-ui'. Each task file confirms its repository: task-1 through task-4 list 'trustify-backend', task-5 through task-10 list 'trustify-ui'. summary-comment.md lines 9-12 reconfirm the Epic-to-task assignments.

assertion_results.text: The plan documents Incorporates links from the Feature to each Epic (not from Feature to individual Tasks) — evidenced by link decisions in the impact map or task output files
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lines 73-76: 'Feature TC-9006 --Incorporates--> Epic 1 (TC-9006: trustify-backend)' and 'Feature TC-9006 --Incorporates--> Epic 2 (TC-9006: trustify-ui)'. Line 76 explicitly states: 'Incorporates links are from Feature to Epics only, NOT from Feature directly to Tasks. Tasks are children of their respective Epics.' summary-comment.md lines 25-28 repeat these links.

assertion_results.text: Task dependency ordering is preserved within and across Epics — backend tasks have lower task numbers than dependent frontend tasks
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md dependency graph (lines 58-70) shows Task 1 -> Task 2 -> Task 3 -> Task 4 (backend, tasks 1-4) and Task 3 -> Task 5 -> Tasks 6,7 -> Task 8 -> Tasks 9,10 (frontend, tasks 5-10). Backend tasks (1-4) all have lower numbers than frontend tasks (5-10). Task 5 (frontend) depends on Task 3 (backend), confirming cross-epic ordering.

assertion_results.text: Each non-documentation, non-testing task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Documentation tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates doc-only scope) and testing tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates cross-cutting testing scope) are exempt from requiring Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes — they must still include Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Non-doc/non-testing tasks (1-9): Task 1 has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Create, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 2 has all required sections. Task 3 has all required sections. Task 4 (testing task) has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Create, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements — exceeds minimum. Task 5 has all required sections. Task 6 has all required sections. Task 7 has all required sections. Task 8 has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 9 (testing task) has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Create, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 10 (documentation task) has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements — meets the exempted minimum.

assertion_results.text: The plan documents Epic creation with the level-1 issue type name ('Epic') and parent set to the feature issue key — evidenced by Epic creation parameters in the impact map or output files
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lines 15-16: 'Issue Type: Epic (level-1)' and 'Parent: TC-9006' for Epic 1. Lines 21-22: same for Epic 2. summary-comment.md lines 9-10: 'Epic 1: TC-9006: trustify-backend (Issue Type: Epic, level-1; Parent: TC-9006)' and lines 11-12: 'Epic 2: TC-9006: trustify-ui (Issue Type: Epic, level-1; Parent: TC-9006)'.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 10 task files contain a '## Repository' section with a single repo name: task-1 through task-4 contain 'trustify-backend'; task-5 through task-10 contain 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 10 task files contain '## Target Branch' (all set to 'main'), '## Description', '## Acceptance Criteria', and '## Test Requirements' sections. Verified in task-1.md through task-10.md.

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: All 10 digest files exist (task-1-digest.md through task-10-digest.md). Each contains '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-hex-chars>'. Examples: task-1-digest.md has 'sha256-md:b175cc13cec2b10523a7e7b61857ecd8159782e4e21d09a4c01dd74f18b4f500', task-10-digest.md has 'sha256-md:11ae7eaee55664233a89c98e6e05f473d2e18b0d7990d0d841ea7735e6dfb376'. All hashes are exactly 64 lowercase hex characters.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: Tasks with conventions include proper rationale format. Task 1: 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §Module pattern...\nApplies: task creates `modules/fundamental/src/remediation/model/summary.rs` matching the convention's `.rs` module file scope.' Task 2: 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §Error handling...\nApplies: task creates `modules/fundamental/src/remediation/service/mod.rs` matching the convention's `.rs` file scope.' Task 3 has two conventions both with proper format. Task 4: 'Applies: task creates `tests/api/remediation.rs` matching the convention's `.rs` file scope.' Task 5: 'Applies: task modifies `src/api/rest.ts` matching the convention's `.ts` file scope.' and 'Applies: task creates `src/hooks/useRemediationSummary.ts` matching the convention's `.ts` file scope.' Tasks 6, 7, 8, 9 all follow the same format. No conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. No free-form prose rationales found.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 6: 'Priority: Major (inherited by all tasks)'. Lines 27-28: 'Priority: Major — inherited from Feature TC-9006, propagated to all Epics and Tasks'. summary-comment.md line 31: 'Priority: Major — inherited from Feature TC-9006, propagated to both Epics and all 10 Tasks'. The priority is 'Major' (not 'Undefined'), so inheritance is documented as propagated.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 7: 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (inherited by all tasks)'. Lines 28: 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 — inherited from Feature TC-9006, propagated to all Epics and Tasks'. No '### Jira Field Defaults' section in CLAUDE.md, so fixVersion scope defaults to 'both'. summary-comment.md line 32: 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 — inherited from Feature TC-9006, propagated to both Epics and all 10 Tasks'.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists and contains '## Inherited Field Propagation' section (lines 30-32) with: 'Priority: Major — inherited from Feature TC-9006, propagated to both Epics and all 10 Tasks' and 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 — inherited from Feature TC-9006, propagated to both Epics and all 10 Tasks'. impact-map.md also has a '## Field Inheritance' section (lines 26-28) documenting the same.
